title: Team Hot Wheels Top Rides Series (2012)
summary: The Team Hot Wheels Top Rides Series was released exclusively at Target stores in 2012. All castings in this series are all 2011 and 2012 mainline releases on a specially designed card by the picks of the four driver teams. Each vehicle comes with a sticker sheet consisting of the Team Hot Wheels logo and the team color icon.

title: Display Case Artwork
summary: Artwork on the front of each case has varied over the years. This is a page to add pictures of the various artwork for different size car cases. Some of these cases where made by Tara Toys.

title: Hot Wheels Elite 1:18 Scale
summary: This is a 1:18 scale series of Hot Wheels cars under the Hot Wheels Elite branding produced from 2006 to 2015. Most castings from 2006 to 2011 have a limited production run of between 5,000 and 10,000. Some models are part of the "Super Elite" line with a significantly reduced production run.

title: Lowrider Magazine 3-Car Set
summary:
500px
The lowrider phenomenon originated in the barrios of Southern California during the late 1940's. This unique folk art quickly spread throughout the communities of the Southwest and into the northern cities of Chicago and Detroit, where it gained national attention and popularity. Lowriders are not about speed and muscle, but style and beauty.

title: List of 2026 Hot Wheels (by Series)
summary: For 2026, the Hot Wheels mainline is numbered 1 through 250 with color variations not receiving a new number. Treasure Hunts and store exclusives are numbered. For the listing by series see List of 2026 Hot Wheels.

title: Aurimat Hot Wheels
summary: In the 1980's, Hot Wheels were manufactured in Mexico by Aurimat. Nearly all the variations were exclusive to Mexico.

title: Austin
summary: The Austin Motor Company Limited was a British manufacturer of motor vehicles, founded in 1905 by Herbert Austin in Longbridge. In 1952, it was merged with Morris Motors Limited to form the new holding company British Motor Corporation (BMC) Limited, while retaining its separate identity. The marque Austin was used by BMC's successors, British Leyland and Rover Group, until 1987.
